title: LOW: Ammonia sanitizer does not restrict URL schemes in href
description: In src/posting.rs lines 296-302, the html_sanitizer() configures ammonia with allowed tags and attributes but does not explicitly call .url_schemes() to restrict which URL schemes are allowed in href attributes. Ammonia's default does block javascript: URLs, so this is not directly exploitable. However, explicitly configuring allowed schemes (e.g., http, https, mailto) would be defense-in-depth against any future ammonia default change or unusual scheme-based attacks (data:, blob:, etc.). File: /home/mark/PROJECT/smallhold/src/posting.rs lines 296-302.
close_reason: Fixed: html_sanitizer() now explicitly restricts URL schemes to http, https, mailto.
